title: cassia setosa var. subsetosa
intro: Cassia setosa var. subsetosa, also known as the forest cassia, is a plant species in the legume family Fabaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow, linear leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cassia setosa var. subsetosa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Cassia setosa var. subsetosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
